skill.name: multi-channel
skill.description: Coordinates social inbox triage, ad campaign review, keyword analysis, and cross-platform messaging for major social channels.
skill.summary: Plan, triage, and optimize social marketing activity across major platforms.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown code fences and marketing terminology as shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance. Manual review found no executable code, no command invocation, no prompt injection, and no data exfiltration pattern in SKILL.md. The remaining risk is operational: the skill describes workflows that may handle social inbox, ad, and analytics data.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the flagged locations are markdown code fence delimiters around documentation examples. No executable command path, shell invocation, Ruby code, or user-controlled command construction was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each listed external command hit is a markdown delimiter in SKILL.md. The skill contains prose and examples, not executable Ruby, shell scripts, or command handlers.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Text Matches Only
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm indicators, but the flagged lines contain marketing and platform guidance words such as description, recommendations, device, content, design, hashtags, and design tools. No cryptographic API, hashing function, cipher selection, password handling, or key material was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged terms appear in ordinary documentation context, not security-sensitive code. There is no evidence of cryptographic implementation or weak algorithm use.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Detections Are Marketing Examples
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported system and network reconnaissance, but the flagged lines describe ad metrics, bid strategy, scheduling, campaign goals, pre-launch budgeting, and user trigger phrases. No host enumeration, network scanning, IP collection, DNS probing, or environment discovery behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are marketing planning examples and natural-language trigger phrases. They do not contain reconnaissance commands, network APIs, or collection logic.

low_findings.title: Sensitive Marketing Data Handling Requires User Controls
low_findings.description: The skill describes social inbox aggregation, advertising dashboards, and third-party API integrations. This is not malicious, but users should avoid pasting private customer messages, ad account identifiers, or analytics exports unless their workspace policy allows it.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The data sensitivity is clear from documented social inbox, ad platform, and analytics workflows. The risk is operational rather than a confirmed vulnerability because the skill contains no implementation code.

content.value_statement: Marketing teams often manage inboxes, ads, keywords, and campaign plans in separate tools. This skill creates one planning workflow for channel-specific messaging, triage, and performance review.
actual_capabilities: Organizes social inbox triage across YouTube, Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, X, Facebook, Reddit, and Pinterest.
Suggests response priorities and draft reply options based on message type and brand voice.
Reviews campaign metrics such as CPM, CPC, CTR, CPA, ROAS, conversions, and engagement.
Builds platform-specific campaign plans with timelines, budgets, messaging, and content formats.
Compares keyword and audience signals to support bid, budget, and targeting decisions.
Summarizes platform best practices for publishing, ads, hashtags, timing, and engagement.
limitations: It does not include working API connectors or authentication flows.
It cannot verify live social, ad, or analytics data without user-provided inputs.
It does not publish posts, send replies, or change ad budgets by itself.
Platform rules and ad products may change after the skill documentation was written.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Social Inbox Work
use_cases.description: Sort comments, mentions, and direct messages by urgency, topic, platform, and recommended response style.

use_cases.title: Review Paid Campaign Performance
use_cases.description: Compare ad metrics across platforms and identify budget, creative, audience, and timing adjustments.

use_cases.title: Plan Coordinated Launch Messaging
use_cases.description: Create a platform-by-platform campaign plan with goals, formats, posting cadence, and consistent calls to action.

prompt_templates.title: Check Inbox Priorities
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these recent comments and messages. Group them by platform, urgency, message type, and suggested next action.

prompt_templates.title: Draft Brand-Safe Replies
prompt_templates.prompt: Create three reply options for each message. Keep the tone helpful, concise, and consistent with this brand voice: [brand voice].

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Campaign Spend
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these campaign metrics by platform. Identify underperforming areas, budget shifts, creative tests, and risks to monitor.

prompt_templates.title: Build Launch Campaign Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a six-week launch plan for [product]. Include platform roles, audience segments, content formats, budget allocation, metrics, and review cadence.

output_examples.input: A community manager asks for urgent social messages from eight platforms.
output_examples.output: A prioritized inbox summary with urgent complaints first, business inquiries second, general feedback third, and low-risk community comments last.

output_examples.input: A marketer provides recent ad metrics for Google, Meta, LinkedIn, and TikTok.
output_examples.output: A performance review that highlights high-ROAS channels, rising acquisition costs, creative fatigue, and suggested budget tests.

output_examples.input: A launch lead asks for coordinated messaging for a new product release.
output_examples.output: A channel plan with platform goals, recommended formats, posting cadence, audience notes, and consistent calls to action.

best_practices: Provide platform, audience, objective, budget, and time range for every campaign review.
Separate private customer data from summary metrics when possible.
Review platform-specific recommendations before publishing or changing budgets.
anti_patterns: Do not paste credentials, access tokens, or full customer records into prompts.
Do not apply generic messaging across every platform without adapting format and tone.
Do not treat generated optimization advice as a substitute for live account verification.

faq.question: Does this skill connect directly to social platforms?
faq.answer: No. The documentation describes workflows and integrations, but it does not include working API connectors.

faq.question: Can it send replies or publish posts automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can help draft and plan responses, but users must send or schedule content in their approved tools.

faq.question: Which platforms are covered?
faq.answer: It covers YouTube, Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, X, Facebook, Reddit, Pinterest, Google Ads, Meta Ads, LinkedIn Ads, and TikTok Ads.

faq.question: What campaign metrics does it use?
faq.answer: It references CPM, CPC, CTR, CPA, ROAS, conversions, engagement, reach, and platform-specific quality indicators.

faq.question: Is it suitable for sensitive customer messages?
faq.answer: Use summaries when possible. Follow your privacy policy before sharing direct messages or customer identifiers.

faq.question: Does it replace a marketing analytics platform?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure analysis and recommendations, but live reporting still requires trusted analytics and ad tools.
